properties.remark: ACCOMPANIED BY VERY HEAVY RAIN.
SMALL UTILITY SHED BLOWN DOWN BY WIND NEAR BLACKDUCK LAKE.
MN DNR REPORTED APPARENT DRY MICROBURST BETWEEN OAK ISLAND AND FLAG ISLAND ON THE LAKE OF THE WOODS. WIND GUST MEASURED AT FGN ASOS.

FARM ACCESS ROADS WERE FLOODED AND 140 ACRE FIELD WAS FLOODED. U.S. HIGHWAY 75 NORTH OF KENT WAS UNDERWATER AND REMAINED CLOSED DUE TO DAMAGE FROM EARLIER SPRING FLOODING. PORTIONS OF SOUTHERN ROBERTS AND NORTHERN MCCAULEYVILLE TOWNSHIPS ALONG THE WHISKEY CREEK DRAINAGE RECEIVED 5 TO 7 INCHES OF RAIN FROM LATE AFTERNOON INTO THE LATE EVENING.

FIELDS AND FARM ACCESS ROADS WERE FLOODED BY REPEATED HEAVY RAINS. PORTIONS OF SOUTHERN BARNESVILLE AND ALLIANCE TOWNSHIPS RECEIVED FROM 3 TO 6 INCHES OF RAIN FROM LATE AFTERNOON THROUGH EVENING HOURS.

FIELDS AND FARM ACCESS ROADS WERE FLOODED NEAR ABERCROMBIE. PORTIONS OF SOUTHERN EAGLE AND NORTHEASTERN ABERCROMBIE TOWNSHIPS RECEIVED FROM 4 TO 7 INCHES OF RAIN FROM THE LATE AFTERNOON THROUGH OVERNIGHT HOURS.
PUBLIC REPORT OF A BRIEF TOUCHDOWN REPORTED TO SHERIFFS OFFICE.
TORNADO TOUCHDOWN REPORTED NEAR TRAVERSE COUNTY LINE.
LOTS OF GOLF BALL AND LARGER HAIL...SIGNIFICANT TREE DAMAGE
HAIL FELL ALONG THE RANSOM AND BARNES COUNTY LINE.
HAIL FELL ALONG THE RANSOM AND BARNES COUNTY LINE.
3 TO 4 INCH DIAMETER BRANCHES BROKEN OFF TREES
CULVERT BLOWN OUT DUE TO EXCESSIVE RAINFALL.
OVERLAND FLOODING OBSERVED DUE TO EXCESSIVE RAIN RUNOFF
ON COUNTY ROAD 8 AND COUNTY ROAD 1
LARGE HAIL CRACKED WINDSHIELDS AND STOPPED SOUTHBOUND TRAFFIC ON I-29 NEAR MP36 FOR A 5 TO 10 MINUTE PERIOD.
ON I-29 NEAR GALCHUTT EXIT AT MP31.
ON I-29 AT GALCHUTT
2 INCH DIA.
UPROOTED TREES

DOWNBURST WINDS TORE DOWN NUMEROUS LARGE BRANCHES AND UPROOTED SEVERAL TREES THROUGH A 4 MILE WIDE PATH EXTENDING FROM 1S OF WYNDMERE TO ABOUT 3N OF TOWN. THE DAMAGE PATH DEVELOPED ABOUT 2W OF WYNDMERE AND CONTINUED EASTWARD THROUGH BARNEY AND MOORETON.
NUMEROUS LARGE BRANCHES AND TREE LIMBS TORN DOWN AND SEVERAL TREES UPROOTED AROUND TOWN.

SEMI TRACTOR AND TRAILER BLOWN OVER ON SOUTHBOUND LANE OF I-29 ABOUT 1 MILE SOUTH OF THE MOORETON SCALE AND ONE QUARTER MILE NORTH OF THE WAHPETON EXIT. THUNDERSTORM DOWNBURST WINDS LIKELY FORMED COINCIDENT WITH A DEVELOPING TORNADO WHICH THEN PASSED JUST NORTH OF THIS INCIDENT AS VIEWED BY SPOTTERS.

A TORNADO DEVELOPED OVER MOORETON AND TRACKED EASTWARD FOR ABOUT 7 MILES THEN DISSIPATED ABOUT 2 SSE OF DWIGHT BY 620 PM CDT. THE TORNADO FLUNG BLEACHERS THROUGH THE CHAIN-LINK BACKDROP OF THE MOORETON BASEBALL FIELD. THE TORNADO AND ACCOMPANYING DOWNBURST WINDS PRODUCED WIDESPREAD DAMAGE TO TREES...ROOFING MATERIALS...SIDING AND TRIM NEAR MOORETON. PEAK WINDS ESTIMATED AT 90 MPH. MAXIMUM TORNADO WIDTH ESTIMATED AT ABOUT 300 YARDS.
MEASURED AT THE WAHPETON RWIS SITE ALONG HIGHWAY 13 AT MP 384.
MEASURED AT THE WAHPETON RWIS SITE ALONG HIGHWAY 13 AT MP 384.
TORNADO REPORTED 1.5 MILES SOUTH OF THE MOORETON SCALES NEAR I-29.
GUSTNADO OBSERVED IN THUNDERSTORM OUTFLOW WELL AHEAD OF AN APPROACHING SHELF CLOUD.
QUARTER SIZED HAIL AND WINDS GUSTS TO 50 MPH REPORTED AT THE ND STATE SCHOOL OF SCIENCE.
SEVERAL LARGE 4-6 INCH TREE BRANCHES BLOWN DOWN.
PEAK WINDS NEAR TENNEY RWIS SITE ON HIGHWAY 75.
TWO SEMI TRUCKS BLOWN OVER ON I-94 BETWEEN ROTHSAY AND FERGUS FALLS EXITS.
SEVERAL TREES BLOWN DOWN IN TOWN.
A TORNADO TRACKED FOR ABOUT 3 MILES TO AROUND 1NW OF ELIZABETH BY 657 PM CDT. PEAK WINDS ESTIMATED AT 80 MPH.
SEMI TRACTOR AND TRAILER BLOWN OFF I-94 NEAR DALTON EXIT.
SEMI TRACTORS AND TRAILERS BLOWN OFF THE INTERSTATE NEAR FERGUS FALLS.
SEVERAL LARGE 4-6 INCH BRANCHES BLOWN DOWN AROUND TOWN.

A TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN BRIEFLY NEAR DENT KNOCKING DOWN SEVERAL LARGE TREES AND RIPING THE ROOF OFF THE DUGOUT AT THE BASEBALL FIELD. THE TORNADO TRACKED FOR ABOUT A MILE AND LASTED UNTIL 717 PM CDT. PEAK WINDS ESTIMATED AT 90 MPH. MAXIMUM TORNADO WIDTH ABOUT 150 YARDS. DOWNBURST WINDS PRODUCED ADDITIONAL WIND DAMAGE AROUND THE BIG MCDONALD AND STAR LAKE AREAS.

A TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN BRIEFLY NEAR DENT KNOCKING DOWN SEVERAL LARGE TREES AND RIPING THE ROOF OFF THE DUGOUT AT THE BASEBALL FIELD. THE TORNADO TRACKED FOR ABOUT A MILE AND LASTED UNTIL 717PM. PEAK WINDS ESTIMATED AT 90 MPH. MAXIMUM TORNADO WIDTH ABOUT 150 YARDS. DOWNBURST WINDS PRODUCED ADDITIONAL WIND DAMAGE AROUND THE BIG MCDONALD AND STAR LAKE AREAS.
SEVERAL 4-6 INCH BRANCHES DOWN AROUND TOWN. LARGE TREE DOWN 1N OF TOWN.
SEVERAL LARGE 6-8 INCH TREE LIMBS DOWN. DAMAGING WINDS LASTED ABOUT 5 MINUTES.
SEVERAL 4-6 INCH TREE BRANCHES BLOWN DOWN.
SEVERAL FIELDS AND FARM ACCESS ROADS FLOODED BETWEEN COLFAX...GALCHUTT AND ABERCROMBIE IN ABERCROMBIE AND SOUTHEAST COLFAX TOWNSHIPS.

SEVERAL FIELDS AND FARM ACCESS ROADS FLOODED IN MCCAULEYVILLE AND NORDICK TOWNSHIPS. U.S. HIGHWAY 75 BRIDGE OVER WHISKEY CREEK CLOSED WITH WATER RUNNING LEVEL AT BRIDGE DECK. HIGHWAY ALSO CLOSED AT RAILROAD BRIDGE UNDERPASS DUE TO LONGTERM FLOODING ISSUES.
FARM FIELDS FLOODED UP TO 4 FEET DEEP IN PLACES IN SOUTH OLGA TOWNSHIP.
WEST SIDE OF TULABY LAKE
HAIL OCCURRED FOR AROUND 5 MINUTES DURING VERY HEAVY RAIN.
A PERSISTENT FUNNEL PRODUCED A COUPLE BRIEF TOUCHDOWNS IN OPEN RANGELAND IN SOUTHERN ARNE TOWNSHIP...NEAR THE WELLS COUNTY LINE.

A TORNADO TRACKED FOR ABOUT 3 MILES TO NEAR 3 NE MICHIGAN BY 539 PM CDT. THE TORNADO REMAINED OVER MAINLY OPEN FIELDS AND RANGELAND. MAXIMUM WIDTH ESTIMATED AT 100 YARDS. PEAK WINDS ETIMATED TO 80 MPH.
LARGE 12 INCH LIMBS BROKEN DOWN. WINDS ESTIMATED TO 60 MPH.
LARGE BRANCHES BREAKING FROM TREES. WINDS ESTIMATED 50-60 MPH.
TREE DOWN
JUNCTION OF ST HW 18 AND 15 TREES DOWN.
HWY 81 WALSH/PEMBINA COUNTY LINE. ESTIMATED WIND SPEEDS 60-70 MPH.

A TORNADO TRACKED TO THE EAST-SOUTHEAST FOR ABOUT 3 MILES TO 1 SW OF ARDOCK BY 638 PM CDT. THE TORNADO BROKE DOWN SEVERAL LARGE TREE LIMBS IN SHELTER BELTS AND PRODUCED A FAIRLY WIDE DEBRIS SWIRL TO THE WEST OF ARDOCH AS VIEWED BY OBSERVERS NEAR THE SOUTHWEST EDGE OF ARDOCH. MAXIMUM WIDTH ESTIMATED AT 200 YARDS. PEAK WINDS ESTIMATED TO 80 MPH.

THIS TORNADO WAS VIEWED BY THE HIGHWAY PATROL JUST TO THE NORTH OF U.S. HWY 2. THE TORNADO TRACKED INTERMITTENTLY EASTWARD FOR NEARLY 7 MILES TO AROUND 5 NNE OF LARIMORE BY ABOUT 647 PM CDT. THE TORNADO WAS VIEWED AND REPORTED BY MULTIPLE SOURCES AND SNAPPED OR UPROOTED NUMEROUS TREES IN SHELTER BELTS. MAXIMUM WIDTH ESTIMATED AT 200 YARDS. PEAK WINDS SPEEDS TO 95 MPH.
ON HWY 2
ROOFING MATERIAL TORN OFF A POTATO WAREHOUSE ON THE SOUTH END OF TOWN.

A TORNADO TRACKED FOR ABOUT 5 MILES TO NEAR 4 NW OF MINTO BY 645 PM CDT. THE TORNADO LIFTED A BARN FROM ITS FOUNDATION AND DESTROYED IT WHILE UPROOTING NUMEROUS TREES IN THE FARMYARD. LATER THE TORNADO BROKE DOWN 3 POWER POLES AND SNAPPED OFF NUMEROUS OTHER TREES ALONG ITS ROUTE. MAXIMUM WIDTH 300 YARDS. PEAK WINDS ESTIMATED TO 105 MPH.
HAIL AND WINDS TO 70 MPH PRODUCED WIDESPREAD CROP AND TREE DAMAGE.

A TORNADO TRACKED EASTWARD NEAR WARSAW AND LIFTED ABOUT 3 ESE OF WARSAW BY 655 PM CDT. THE TORNADO SNAPPED OFF NUMEROUS LARGE TREE LIMBS AND BLEW DOWN A STEEL 3K BUSHEL GRAIN BIN ALONG ITS PATH. MAXIMUM WIDTH ESTIMATED AT 250 YARDS. PEAK WINDS ESTIMATED AT 90 MPH.
THIS TORNADO HAS BEEN REPORTED BY MULTIPLE SPOTTERS.

SEVERAL LARGE 4-6 INCH BRANCHES BLOKEN DOWN IN SHELTER BELT. NDAWN WIND SENSOR 2 MILES AWAY MEASURED PEAK WINDS TO 55 MPH.

A TORNADO TRACKED FOR ABOUT 3 MILES TO 2 ESE OF CUMMINGS BY 920 PM CDT. SEVERAL LINDEN TREES WERE BROKEN DOWN IN SHELTER BELTS. PEAK WINDS ESTIMATED TO 80 MPH.
MEASURED AT THE ND DOT RWIS SITE ALONG 1-29 NEAR MP114.

A TORNADO TRACKED EASTWARD FOR ABOUT 1 MILE TO 2 ESE OF BUXTON BY 923 PM CDT. A 60X120 STORAGE BUILDING WAS DESTROYED AND ROOFING MATERIALS WERE PEELED UP ON A POTATO WAREHOUSE. SEVERAL PINE AND POPLAR TREES WERE SNAPPED OFF. MAXIMUM TORNADO WIDTH TO 100 YDS. PEAK WINDS ESTIMATED TO 95 MPH.
A TORNADO TRACKED FOR ABOUT A MILE TO NEAR CALEDONIA BY 953 PM CDT. SEVERAL TREE LIMBS WERE BROKEN DOWN AND A FEW SHINGLES WERE TORN OFF OF HOMES. PEAK WINDS ESTIMATED TO 80 MPH.
STATE HIGHWAY 32 WAS WASHED OUT JUST NORTH OF THE INTERSECTION WITH COUNTY 1. SEVERAL OTHER WASHOUTS OCCURED ON COUNTY ROADS IN ELKMONT AND INKSTER TOWNSHIPS.
FILEDS AND FARM ROADS WERE FLOODED ACORSS PORTIONS OFSTAVANGER...ERVIN...BELMONT AND BINGHAM TOWNSHIPS IN NORTHEAST TRAILL COUNTY. SEVERAL COUNTY ROADS WERE WASHED OUT OR BLOCKED BY FLOWING WATERS.
FILEDS AND FARM ROADS WERE FLOODED ACROSS NORTHER NASH AND EASTERN DAHLEN TOWNSHIPS. COUNTY ROADS WERE WASHED OUT IN NUMEROUS LOCATIONS.
OVERLAND FLOODING WASHED OUT PORTIONS OF THE RAILROAD BED NEAR TOWN. STREET FLOODING AND SOME BUILDING FLOODING OCCURED IN TOWN THROUGH THE OVERNIGHT HOURS.
ND-ARB OBSERVER REPORTED BRIEF TOUCHDOWN NORTH OF THEIR LOCATION. ANOTHER FUNNEL VISIBLE TO THE EAST BUT LOWER ELEVATIONS OBSCURED BY TREE LINE.
MEASURED BY OBSERVERS DAVIS UNIT.
